english: it has struck my eyes, I should like to have it C., W.; *mig log ltá-ce* (mig log lta ce) to eye one obliquely, with envy or jealousy W. - Comp. mig-kyóg (mig kyog) squinting Sch. - mig-rkyén (mig rkyen) Mil., is said to be the same as mig-ltós. (mig ltos) - *mig-skyór* (mig skyor) W. eye-ball. - mig-skyág (mig skyag) the impurities in the eyes Cs. - mig-kúṅ (mig khung) eye-hole, socket Sch.. - mig-°prul (mig 'phrul) Mil. v. mig-°prul. (mig 'phrul) - mig-grogs (mig grogs) one's sweetheart Cs. - mig-gram (mig gram) edge of the eye Sch. - mig-rgyaṅ (mig rgyang) 1. v. rgyaṅ-ma. (rgyang ma) 2. far-sightedness, mig-rgyaṅ-ćan (mig rgyang can) one that is far-sighted, mig rgyaṅ-túṅ (mig rgyang thung) short-sighted Bhar. mig-sgyu (mig sgyu) mirage, looming, Fata Morgana, sós-kai táṅ-la mig-sgyu gyú-ba bźin (sos ka'i thang la mig sgyu rgyu ba bzhin) Thgr. like the mirage on a plain in the hot season. - mig-sgyur-ma (mig sgyur ma) = mká-°gro-ma (mkha' 'gro ma) Mil. - mig-ćan (mig can) 1. having eyes. 2. having seeds or grains, fructified, of ears of corn W. - mig-ćer (mig cer) v. će-re. (ce re) - mig-lćibs (mig lcibs) eye-lid Med. - mig-ću (mig chu) 1. tears W. 2. hydrophthalmia Med. 3. mig-ću dzág-pa (mig chu 'dzag pa) blear-eyes Schr. - mig-brnyás kyér-ba (mig brnyas khyer ba) Mil. c. dat., to slight, to treat contemptuously. - mig-rtúl (mig rtul) dim, dull eyes Sch. - mig-lta (mig lta) (resp. źál-lta, źál-ta (zhal lta, zhal ta)) byéd-pa (byed pa) to inspect, superintend (*mig-ta-kán* (mig lta mkhan) overseer of workmen); to keep, to guard; to care for, to minister, to serve. - mig-ltág (mig ltag) Sch. = mig-skyág(?) (mig skyag) - mig-ltós (mig ltos) 1. eye-sight, look, mien Cs. 2. C. W. learning by observation and close ocular attention, *gár-źa-pe hín-dui mig-tós kúr, or kyon, or lob dug* (gar zha pa'i hin du'i mig ltos khur, or kyon, or slob 'dug) W. the people of Lahoul copy the Hindoos; *mig-tós ṅán-pa kúr, or lob soṅ* (mig ltos ngan pa khur, or slob song) W. he has imitated what is not good. - *mig-tọ́-la pém-pa, or ṅọ́-pa* (mig ltos la dpe ba, or ngo pa) C. to derive profit or harm from observing and imitating others(?) *mig-tọ́-la pém-pe ṭim* (mig ltos la dpe ba'i khrims) deterring punishment. - *mig-ṭág tóṅ-wa* (mig ltag gtong ba) a kind of torture in C., little hooks, connected by strings, being fastened in the lower eye-lids as well as in the chest, by which means the former are constantly drawn down and prevented from closing. - mig-túṅ (mig thung) short-sightedness Cs., mig-túṅ-ćan (mig thung can) short-sighted. - *mig-śla* (mig shla) snow-spectacles, shades formed of a texture of horsehair. - *mig-dól* (mig rtol) C. = rnyid-rdól. (rnyid rdol) - mig-ldán (mig ldan) = mig-ćan (mig can) po. needle. - mig-nád, (mig nad) disease of the eye. - mig-po (mig po) = mig (mig) Cs., mig-po-ćé (mig po che) a large eye Cs. - *mig-pág* (mig pags) C., W. eye-lid. - mig-spriṅ (mig spring) 'a white spot in the eye' Sch.; acc. to Lt. it seems to be the white of the eye, sclerotica, in C. the cataract is called so. - mig-pór (mig phor) Cs. = mig-kúṅ. (mig khung) - mig-°krúl (mig 'khrul) Mil. optical deception, mig-°krúl-mkán (mig 'khrul mkhan) a showman Cs. - mig-bu (mig bu) 'Augenklappe' Sch. (?) - mig-°búr (mig 'bur) goggle-eyes. - mig-°brás (mig 'bras) apple of the eye, eye-ball, *mig-dás lóg-će, or mig-kór lóg-će* (mig das log ce, or mig skor log ce) W. to roll the eyes; bdág-gi mig-gi °bras ltar gćés-na yaṅ (bdag gi mig gi 'bras ltar gces na yang) although she is as dear to me as the apple of my eye. - mig-máṅ(s) (mig mangs) chess-board, game at tables, mig-máṅ rtsé-ba (mig mang rtse ba) Dzl. to play at chess, mig-maṅ-rís-su bris-pa (mig mang ris su bris pa) Glr. chequered, painted or in-laid work after the pattern of a chess-board. - mig-méd (mig med) eyeless, blind. - mig-dmár (mig dmar) 1. red eye, as a symptom of disease Lt. 2. the planet Mars. - mig-smán (mig sman) eye-medicine. - mig-rtsa (mig rtsa) 1. prob. Vena facialis externa Med. 2. the blood-vessels of the sclerotica, mig-rtsa krúgs-pa (mig rtsa 'khrugs pa) the blood-vessels irritated, reddened Med. - *mig-sál* (mig gsal) W. sharp-sightedness, *mig-sál-kán* (mig gsal mkhan) sharp-sighted, *mig-sal-nyám* (mig gsal nyams) the contrary. - mig-rtség (mig rtseg) the wrinkles of the eye-lid Cs. - mig-tsíl, (mig tshil) 1. fat in the eye Mil. 2. the white in the eye Cs. - *mig-tsig(-ćè)* (mig tshig ce) W. inflammation of the eye, *ká-mig-tsig* (kha mig tshig) caused by snow, *dúd-mig-tsig* (dud mig tshig) caused by smoke. - mig-zí (mig zi) mist before the eyes Sch. - mig-zúr (mig zur) corner of the eye Sch. - mig-gzúgs (mig gzugs) S.g. optical perception, a picture of objects being formed on the retina by reflected rays of light (merely guessed by Tibetan science, not ascertained by observation and research). - *mig-yáṅ(s)* (mig yangs) C., W. liberal, bountiful. - mig-yór, (mig yor) 1. Sch. = mig-rtúl. (mig rtul) 2. = mig-sgyú (mig sgyu) Thgr. - mig-rig-rig (mig rig rig) Mil. timidly, anxiously looking to and fro, hither and thither. - mig-ríṅ-ćan (mig ring can) = mig-rgyáṅ-ćan (mig rgyang can) Cs. - mig-rís (mig ris) artificial eye-brows Cs. - mig-rús (mig rus) eye-bone Cs. - mig-slóbs (mig slobs) the act of accustoming the eyes to.., mig-slóbs ṅán-pa skye (mig slobs ngan pa skye) Mil. you habituate yourself to a faulty look, i. e.
